title: corydalis oxypetala subsp. oxypetala
intro: Corydalis oxypetala subsp. oxypetala, also known as the Himalayan corydalis, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the Himalayas and adjacent regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine and protopine. These medicinal properties make it beneficial for treating conditions like pain, insomnia, and anxiety, as well as for improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation. Corydalis oxypetala subsp. oxypetala is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule for its medicinal use, with some herbal preparations also incorporating it into formulas for improved sleep quality and relaxation.
